guide:
How to interpret the response:
- result.total_items is the total number of instruments matching the scan's filters. result.results may contain fewer entries if the scan's frontend_limit caps the rows — mention this to the user when total_items > len(results).
- result.results is an array of matching instruments. Each entry has:
  - ticker: trading symbol (e.g. "AAPL"). Empty when the scan does not have a Symbol/Ticker column visible — use instrument_id then.
  - instrument_id: Robinhood instrument UUID. Pass this to other trading tools that take an instrument_id.
  - instrument_type: STOCK, ETF, etc.
  - columns: map of column display name -> cell value. Values are pre-formatted strings from upstream — re-format only if needed for the user.
- result.sorted_by tells you the row order (e.g. "Volume desc"). Mention it when describing the table.
- result.filters_applied lists the filters in effect — useful for confirming "this scan currently filters by RSI > 70".
- result.cortex_managed: true means the scan was created by Cortex inside Legend. Reads are fine, but update_scan_filters / update_scan_config will reject Cortex-managed scans — tell the user that if they ask to modify it.

When presenting results:
- Render as a table with ticker as the leftmost column followed by the most user-relevant columns (% Change, Volume, etc.).
- State total_items so the user knows how big the match set is.
- Note that results are live as of execution time — they will differ if run again later.
- If results is empty: tell the user the scan returned 0 matches right now. Suggest relaxing filters with update_scan_filters if they want broader results.

Error handling:
- "scan does not exist or you do not have access to it" → the scan_id is wrong or the scan belongs to a different user. Suggest get_scans to see available scans.
- Rate-limited or upstream failure → surface the error verbatim, no automatic retry.
